_meta.note: Built to the Armagh-locked standard. Offaly, relegated from Division 2 and humbled by Laois in the Leinster opener, reinvented their season under Mickey Harte and Declan Kelly with a three-game Tailteann Cup winning run (Clare, a major upset of favourites Down, and Wexford) to reach the semi-final, where Wicklow overturned an eight-point half-time deficit to beat them. Five championship games.
_meta.verification: All scorelines, venues and referees verified against primary match reports and official tables (RTE, gaa.ie, The42, Irish Examiner, Irish News, HoganStand, The Journal). Full scorer lists held for the Clare R1 win, the Wexford QF win and the Wicklow semi-final loss; partial for the Down R2A upset.

manager.name: Mickey Harte and Declan Kelly
manager.note: Joint management. Inherited a side mired in a nine-game losing run (seven league, plus the Leinster loss to Laois) and a Division 2 relegation, and turned the season around with three straight Tailteann wins, including a statement defeat of tournament favourites Down, to reach the semi-final.

source.snippet: Mickey Harte and Declan Kelly's side ended a run of nine successive defeats as they bounced back from Division Two league relegation and a disappointing Leinster championship loss to Laois with a three-point win in Ennis.

league.note: Suffered a bitterly disappointing Division 2 relegation, part of a run of seven straight league defeats that bled into the championship.

source.snippet: they bounced back from Division Two league relegation and a disappointing Leinster championship loss to Laois.

results.note: A nine-point Leinster opening loss to Laois (described in reports as a 'championship humiliation'), the low point of a season then mired in a nine-game losing run. Sent Offaly into the Tailteann Cup. Full scorer list not captured; to be filled on a verification pass.

results.venue: Zimmer Biomet Páirc Chíosóg (Cusack Park), Ennis

scorers.note: 1 two-point free, 2 two-pointers from play; the talisman
goal that cancelled out Cormac Murray's for Clare

source.snippet: Scorers for Offaly: D Hyland 0-9 (1tpf, 2tp); S Tierney 1-0; Keith O'Neill, Marcas Dalton, Harry Plunkett 0-2 each; Jordan Hayes, Nathan Poland 0-1 each.

results.note: Offaly's first competitive win of 2026, ending a nine-defeat run. Powered into a 0-9 to 0-4 lead with the wind (Hyland two-pointers key), survived a Clare fightback (Cormac Murray goal and two-pointer put Clare 1-8 to 1-6 up), then closed with four unanswered points. Full scorer list. Offaly 3 two-pointers, Clare 2. Clare's perspective is held in Clare's file.

scorers.note: late clinching goal off a rebound

source.snippet: Offaly produced a brilliant display to defeat favourites Down by 3-22 to 1-20 in this evening's Tailteann Cup Round 2A clash in Tullamore. Dylan Hyland... hit 1-11... including a 16th minute penalty... Two-pointers by Keith O'Neill and Jordan Hayes... a Shane Tierney goal... a Diarmuid Egan goal late on.

results.note: The signature win of Offaly's season: a comprehensive eight-point defeat of tournament favourites (and eventual finalists) Down, advancing Offaly directly to the quarter-final. Dylan Hyland's 1-11 (including a penalty) the centrepiece, with goals from Shane Tierney and a late Diarmuid Egan, and two-pointers from Keith O'Neill and Jordan Hayes. Partial scorer list (the three goalscorers, Hyland's 1-11 and the two two-pointers itemised; full breakdown not captured). Down's perspective will live in Down's file (Down still alive, Tailteann finalist).

scorers.note: clinical 41st-minute goal that turned the game

source.snippet: Offaly: ... Shane O'Toole-Greene (0-3)... Jack McEvoy (0-3)... Dylan Hyland (0-2, 2f)... Jack Bryant (1-3). Subs: Harry Plunkett (0-4, 1 x 2p)...

results.note: A third straight win, from six points down at half-time (Wexford led 1-13 to 0-10). Offaly outscored Wexford 1-12 to 0-4 in the second half, Jack Bryant's 41st-minute goal the turning point, to reach a first Tailteann semi-final since 2022. Full scorer list. Offaly 2 two-pointers (Plunkett, plus one). Wexford's perspective is held in Wexford's file. Referee per primary report.

scorers.note: top scorer; 57th-minute goal that briefly levelled it
goal seconds after Wicklow's opener, rounding the keeper
powered through the centre to finish

source.snippet: Offaly scorers: Jack McEvoy 1-4, Dylan Hyland 1-0, Marcas Dalton 1-0, Shane Tierney 1-0, Keith O'Neill 0-4 (0-1f), Jordan Hayes 0-3, Jack Bryant 0-2, Harry Plunkett 0-1, Rory Egan 0-1.

results.note: Elimination game and a painful one. Offaly raised four goals (Hyland, Dalton, Tierney before half-time, then McEvoy on 57) and led by eight at the break, 3-11 to 1-9, but Wicklow outscored them 1-17 to 0-4 in the second half (a goalkeeper Conor Melia error among the swings) to win by five. Offaly hit no two-pointers; Wicklow hit five. Full scorer list. Wicklow's perspective is held for Wicklow's file (Wicklow still alive, Tailteann finalist). Referee: Seán Lonergan (Tipperary).

collection_meta.collected_by: Claude (research session, not yet hand-verified by Rory)
fields_not_found: Full scorer list for the Leinster Round 1 loss to Laois and the full per-player breakdown of the Down Round 2A upset (key scorers itemised; the rest not captured)
collection_meta.notes: Offaly is one of the stronger Tailteann narratives of 2026 and a good mid-weight Leinster capsule, arguably chapter-adjacent: a county nine games into a losing streak and freshly relegated who, under Mickey Harte and Declan Kelly, beat Clare, then stunned favourites Down, then came from six down to beat Wexford, before leading the Wicklow semi-final by eight at half-time only to be reeled in. Dylan Hyland is the talisman (0-9 v Clare, 1-11 v Down) with Jack McEvoy, Jack Bryant, Shane Tierney, Marcas Dalton and Keith O'Neill the supporting cast. The Down upset (3-22 to 1-20) is a signature-panel candidate; the Wicklow collapse is the bleak counterpoint. A verification pass should fill the Laois and Down scorer lists.
